summary: PR 1973 advanced the lbheap decompilation by replacing placeholder field names with a clearer heap-state model, implementing lbHeap_80015900, and slightly improving nearby heap-status/reporting routines. It also tightened prototypes and added a CameraBlurData struct in lb_00F9 headers, plus a small mndatadel comment. The strongest evidence is in the diff and decomp.dev bot report: lbHeap_80015900 improved from 0.00% to 81.08%, lbHeap_80015F3C from 95.63% to 96.29%, and lbHeap_80015DF8 from 99.63% to 99.88%. There was no human review feedback in the slice.
what_changed: Implemented lbHeap_80015900, including destruction/recreation of transient heap entries, recalculation of main arena and ARAM bounds, creation of HSD main heap and ARAM-backed heap handles, and restoration of persistent heaps.
Renamed the global heap-state struct from lbHeap_80431FA0_t to lbHeap_HeapState and its array field from unk_10 to heap_array.
Renamed heap entry fields from unk-style placeholders to semantic names: id, handle, start, size, type, transient, and status.
Renamed the static heap configuration table fields from unk0/unk4/unk8/unkC to idx/type/prev_idx/size.

Updated existing lbheap routines to use the new Heap struct names: lbHeap_800158D0, lbHeap_800158E8, lbHeap_80015BB8, lbHeap_80015BD0, lbHeap_80015CA8, lbHeap_80015D6C, lbHeap_80015DF8, and lbHeap_80015F3C.
Kept size assertions for the renamed structs: struct Heap remains 0x1C and struct lbHeap_HeapState remains 0xB8.
Improved lb_00F9 prototypes by replacing UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS for lb_80012994 and fn_80013614 with typed signatures using HSD_ImageDesc and HSD_GObj.
Added struct CameraBlurData to lb_00F9.static.h with float fields, u8 fields, an HSD_GObjEvent callback, an HSD_ImageDesc pointer, and padding for layout.
Added baselib/forward.h include to lb_00F9.static.h and placeholder.h include to lbheap.c.
Added a brief comment in mndatadel.c identifying mnDataDel_8024EEC0 as creating the warning modal.
smart_moves: Converted anonymous heap-state offsets into named fields before or alongside matching work, making heap logic substantially easier to reason about without changing asserted layout sizes.

Separated GameCube OS heap usage from lbMemory handle usage through the Heap.type field, clarifying when to call HSD_GetHeap/HSD_SetHeap/HSD_MemAlloc/HSD_Free versus lbMemory allocation/free functions.
Preserved static assertions after renaming structs, reducing risk that improved names accidentally changed ABI/layout assumptions.
Documented uncertainty directly in lbHeap_80015900 with a @todo about the arena-bound loop not being entirely right, rather than overstating confidence.
Used a configuration table with idx, type, prev_idx, and size names to make lbHeap_80015F3C's heap placement logic readable and to expose the dependency between current and previous heap entries.
Tightened header prototypes for lb_00F9 functions, which can help later matching by forcing call sites to carry concrete argument types instead of generic placeholders.
decomp_lessons: When a global manager struct is still named by address, retaining the address-bearing variable name while giving the type and fields semantic names is a low-risk incremental improvement.

For heap or memory-manager code, field names such as start, size, type, status, id, handle, and transient can unlock understanding across many small wrapper functions even before every routine is fully matched.
Do not remove layout safeguards during cleanup: STATIC_ASSERTs are valuable when replacing unk fields with inferred names.
If a loop is structurally matching but semantically suspicious, leave an explicit @todo explaining the suspected control-flow or indexing issue for future reviewers.
Prototype cleanup in headers should be treated as decomp progress: replacing UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS with concrete HSD types can improve call-site readability and future match attempts.
Heap code often has two allocation backends; matching should track which type value selects OS/HSD heap APIs versus custom lbMemory handle APIs.
naming_conventions: Use struct Heap for individual heap entries in lbheap instead of address-derived names like lbHeap_80431FA0_x10_t when the role is understood.
Use struct lbHeap_HeapState for the global heap manager type while retaining the static variable name lbHeap_80431FA0.
Prefer heap_array over offset-style names such as unk_10 for arrays of heap entries.
Prefer arena_lo/arena_hi and aram_lo/aram_hi for memory bounds instead of unk_0/unk_4/unk_8/unk_C.
Prefer idx, type, prev_idx, and size for heap layout configuration table fields instead of unk0/unk4/unk8/unkC.
Use x-offset field names in newly introduced structs like CameraBlurData when the layout is known but semantic names are not yet evidenced.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Implemented a previously stubbed function, lbHeap_80015900, reaching 81.08% according to the decomp.dev report.
Used explicit pointer casts and address arithmetic for arena and ARAM bound calculations, e.g. heap start plus size and comparisons against arena_lo/arena_hi/aram_lo.
Preserved interrupt masking around allocation/free/status operations with OSDisableInterrupts and OSRestoreInterrupts.
Temporarily switches the current HSD heap with HSD_GetHeap and HSD_SetHeap when allocating/freeing from OS/HSD heap-backed entries.
For non-OS heap entries, routes allocation and freeing through lbMemory_80014FC8, lbMemory_800150F0, lbMemory_8001529C, lbMemory_80014E24, and lbMemory_80014EEC.
Maintained explicit sentinel values such as id = -1 and handle = (Handle*) -1 during heap-state initialization.
The bot report recorded minor matching gains for lbHeap_80015F3C and lbHeap_80015DF8 after renaming and logic cleanup.
review_feedback: No human review comments or reviews were present in the provided PR slice.
Automated decomp.dev report showed three unmatched-function improvements: lbHeap_80015900 from 0.00% to 81.08%, lbHeap_80015F3C from 95.63% to 96.29%, and lbHeap_80015DF8 from 99.63% to 99.88%.
follow_up_queries: Investigate the @todo in lbHeap_80015900 about the arena-bound loop checking heaps 2 and 3, then 2 and 1, and determine the intended index pattern.
Continue matching lbHeap_80015900 beyond 81.08% using the now-named Heap and lbHeap_HeapState fields.
Verify whether Heap.type values 0, 1, 2, 3, and 4 can be converted into a named enum without harming matching.
Look for other uses of lbHeap_80431FA0 and lbHeap_803BA380 to confirm the inferred field names id, handle, start, size, type, transient, status, idx, and prev_idx.
Expand CameraBlurData field names once call sites or asset data clarify the meanings of x0, x4, x8, xC, x10, x11, x12, x18, x1C, and x20.
